title: Today's Tech Brief: From 15 hours to one minute: How AI/ML is speeding up GM's development

body.text: I'm looking at a fascinating story from Arstechnica about how General Motors is leveraging AI and machine learning to significantly speed up its development process. Apparently, what used to take 15 hours can now be done in just one minute. That's quite a reduction in time, if you ask me. This got me thinking about the broader implications of AI and ML in the automotive industry.

The technology in question revolves around AI and machine learning applications in the automotive industry, specifically in the development phase. Traditionally, certain simulations and analyses would take a considerable amount of time, often up to 15 hours. With AI/ML, GM has managed to cut this down dramatically to just one minute. This is not just a matter of saving time; it's also about increasing efficiency and potentially accelerating the innovation cycle.
More News: SpaceX's Starlink and EU's Digital Identity

In other news, SpaceX's Starlink is making strides in providing global internet coverage, with recent launches expanding its satellite constellation. Meanwhile, the EU is moving forward with its digital identity initiative, aiming to create a unified digital identity for its citizens. These developments are interesting in their own right, but what's striking is how they're all contributing to a sense of rapid progress in tech.
